title: Agent-facing CLI ergonomics: gate verdicts, --json contract, and workflow primitives
description:
Improve jit's agent-facing ergonomics based on session mining of 42 real Claude Code transcripts across this repo and ../gf2 (jit's primary downstream consumer). 209 friction findings from 37 sessions.

## Top failure patterns
1. `jit gate pass` exit code does not reflect the gate verdict (21 sessions) — agents cannot branch on $?, get silently misled, must follow every pass with a separate gate check.
2. `gate pass` on failure hides checker output, forcing a mandatory `gate check` follow-up (18 sessions).
3. Unstable `--json` schema: short_id missing on fresh/done issues, envelope keys differ per command, gate-check stdout nesting varies (18 sessions; Python KeyError crashes).
4. `gate check`/`check-all` bury the verdict in prose; no structured verdict field (16 sessions).
5. `jit validate` errors on isolated terminal-state issues and cannot be scoped (10 sessions).
6. `gate list`/`gate status` reject an issue ID (9 sessions).
7. `gate pass` no-ops silently when already passed; no pass-all (8 sessions).

## Top unintuitive tool sequencing
1. No `jit gate wait` — agents poll with bespoke sleep+python loops, guessed 45-220s durations (15 sessions).
2. `issue claim` blocks on incomplete deps; error omits `issue assign` (5 sessions).
3. `claim release` needs the opaque lease UUID, not the issue ID; no --agent-id (3 sessions).
4. `issue delete` leaves dangling edges; `validate --fix` won't repair them (2 sessions).
5. No batch issue creation with DAG wiring — agents write hundred-line Python loops (3 sessions).

## Other
Pervasive python3 reshaping of --json (25+ sessions, no --field flag); gates_required/gates_status split; gate-check filesystem spelunking; noisy idempotent gate add; recover false-positive monitors; search missing --label; undiscoverable JIT_ALLOW_DELETION; alias gaps; create --gate possibly no-op; missing gate runs; graph deps --transitive/--flat missing; cargo-ci floods reviewer context.

Full analysis with verbatim evidence and per-item fixes: dev/studies/session-mining-jit-improvements.md

This epic is the parent for breakdown into prioritized work items (see the 12 prioritized recommendations in the study doc). Some findings predate 0.2.1 and must be re-verified against the current binary before implementation.
